2606.12287.title: SpikeDecoder: Realizing the GPT Architecture with Spiking Neural Networks
2606.12287.paper_summary: The Transformer architecture is widely regarded as the most powerful tool for natural language processing, but due to a high number of complex operations, it inherently faces the issue of high energy consumption. To address this issue, we consider Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs), which are an energy-efficient alternative to conventional Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) due to their naturally event-driven approach to processing information. However, this inherently makes them difficult to train. Often, many SNN-based models circumvent this issue by converting pre-trained ANNs. More recently, attempts have been made to design directly trainable SNN-based adaptations of the Transformer model structure. Although the results showed great promise, the application field was computer vision. Moreover, the proposed model incorporates only encoder blocks. In this paper, we propose SpikeDecoder, a fully SNN-based implementation of the Transformer decoder block, for applications in natural language processing. In a series of experiments, we analyze the impact of exchanging different blocks of the ANN model with spike-based alternatives to identify trade-offs and significant sources of performance loss. We further investigate the role of residual connections and the selection of SNN-compatible normalization techniques. Besides the work on the model architecture, we formulate and compare different embedding methods to project text data into spikes. Finally, we demonstrate that our proposed SNN-based decoder block reduces the theoretical energy consumption by 87% to 93% compared to the ANN baseline.
2606.12287.paper_summary_zh: Transformer架構被廣泛認為是自然語言處理中最強大的工具，但由於複雜操作的數量較高，它內在上面臨著高能耗的問題。為了解決這個問題，我們考慮了尖峰神經網絡（SNNs），因為它們以自然事件驅動的方式處理信息，成為傳統人工神經網絡（ANNs）的節能替代方案。然而，這使得它們本質上難以訓練。通常，許多基於SNN的模型通過轉換預訓練的ANN來繞過這個問題。最近，已經有嘗試直接設計可訓練的基於SNN的Transformer模型結構的改編。儘管結果顯示出極大的潛力，但應用領域是計算機視覺。此外，所提出的模型僅包含編碼器塊。在本文中，我們提出了SpikeDecoder，這是一個完全基於SNN的Transformer解碼器塊實現，用於自然語言處理的應用。在一系列實驗中，我們分析了用尖峰基替代品交換ANN模型的不同塊的影響，以識別權衡和顯著的性能損失來源。我們進一步研究了殘差連接的作用以及選擇SNN兼容的正則化技術。除了對模型架構的研究外，我們還制定並比較了不同的嵌入方法，以將文本數據投影為尖峰。最後，我們證明我們提出的基於SNN的解碼器塊將理論能耗減少了87%到93%，與ANN基線相比。
2606.12287.author: Claas Beger et.al.
2606.12287.authors: Claas Beger, Florian Walter, Alois Knoll
